title: fix can't run by source code
body: the error is:    fix NoMatches: No nodes match <class 'textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog'> on XHSDownloader

step_plan.summary: Fix the Textual TUI crash (`NoMatches` for `RichLog`) by removing early widget queries from `XHSDownloader.on_mount()` and performing settings validation/logging only after the Index screen has mounted its `RichLog`. Optionally make `Settings.check_keys()` UI-agnostic for robustness across entry points and Textual timing.

steps.operation: Refactor app startup to stop querying RichLog during App mount; only install/push screens and defer settings/UI warnings to the Index screen
steps.reason: `self.screen.query_one(RichLog)` is timing-sensitive during `App.on_mount()`; depending on Textual scheduling, the active screen may not yet have composed/mounted `RichLog`, raising `NoMatches`. Removing the widget query from `App.on_mount()` eliminates the crash path when running from source.
steps.side_effects: Settings validation/warnings will no longer appear during the earliest app mount phase; ensure the Index screen performs the validation so users still see warnings. Any code relying on warnings being emitted before the first screen is shown may observe a slight delay (typically one frame).

steps.operation: Move settings key validation + user-facing warning log into Index mount, using the Index-owned RichLog instance (e.g., `self.tip`) after it is available
steps.reason: `Index.on_mount()` is the reliable lifecycle point where the Index widget tree exists; querying `RichLog` from within the screen (or using an already-initialized `self.tip`) avoids `NoMatches` and keeps the warning visible in the intended UI location.
steps.side_effects: The warning will appear when the Index screen mounts (after it becomes active) rather than during `App.on_mount()`. If Index can be revisited multiple times, ensure the validation/logging is idempotent or guarded to avoid duplicate warnings.

steps.operation: Harden settings validation to be UI-optional: return missing/invalid key info (or accept nullable logger) instead of requiring an immediate UI node/callback target
steps.reason: Decoupling validation from UI prevents similar timing issues elsewhere and makes the API usable from both TUI and non-TUI entry points. Index can call `check_keys()` to get results and then write to `RichLog` when ready, instead of passing a widget-dependent callback.
steps.side_effects: This is an API behavior change: update all callers to handle the new return value / optional logger. Add or adjust tests or runtime checks to ensure missing-keys detection remains the same and warnings are still emitted in the TUI.
